rationale: The two papers are closely aligned conceptually: the citing paper studies selective choice/attribute non-attendance in conjoint design and estimation, while the cited paper develops a two-stage model to recover unobserved consideration sets from choices. This makes the cited work a plausible mechanism/foundational prior source rather than an incidental mention. Even though the raw evidence is only a single DOI match (no quote context), the topical overlap is strong enough to warrant full relation judgment.
